question: Is the greenhouse interior background (glass panes, sunlight, wet floor) rendered in a photorealistic style with realistic depth of field?
Is the female gardener rendered in a Ukiyo-e woodblock print style, featuring flat color fills and visible color registration edges?
Is the female gardener depicted bending down in a posture consistent with watering a plant?
Is the hummingbird rendered in a stained glass style with visible black lead cames and transparent/translucent colored segments?
Is the hummingbird hovering in front of the female gardener?
Is the rabbit rendered in a watercolor style with visible paper texture or bleeding color edges?
Is the rabbit partially hidden behind a flower pot with only about half of its face visible and obscured by leaves?
Is the LEGO minifigure rendered with a realistic plastic toy texture, exhibiting injection molding seams or hard specular highlights?
Is the LEGO minifigure holding a watering can?
Are the water droplets sprayed by the LEGO figure rendered in a photorealistic style (distinct from the plastic LEGO style)?
Is the body of the cat rendered in a photorealistic real-world style?
Does the cat's tail transform into a traditional Chinese ink wash painting style (dry brush strokes, flying white, or ink bleeding)?
Do the stylized subjects cast realistic shadows onto the photorealistic ground, consistent with unified lighting?
Are clear occlusion relationships visible among the subjects (e.g., leaves obscuring rabbit, hummingbird in front of gardener)?
Are all five distinct subjects (gardener, hummingbird, rabbit, LEGO figure, cat) recognizable in the image?
Is the image free of any readable text?
